# Startup Data Room Checklist: The 60-Document Standard (2026)

## Summary
Peony's 2026 data room guide proposes a 60-document standard organized into consistent folders with date-versioned naming conventions. Reinforces the 7-category structure from the AS Banka source but adds emphasis on file naming conventions (include dates and version numbers), consistent cross-document financial numbers, and a clear index document as the entry point. Confirms that document disorganization is a primary deal-killer and that organized rooms materially compress timelines. Adds specificity on what investors read first: the cap table comes before financial statements in investor review priority.

## Key quotations / statistics
- "60-Document Standard" as an industry benchmark for Series A
- "Use consistent file naming conventions with dates and version numbers"
- "Include a clear index document"
- "Keep financial numbers consistent across all materials"
- "Organize documents into logical, clearly-labeled folders"
- Cap table is reviewed first by investors, before financial statements
- "Median seed round takes 3-6 months; a ready data room compresses that by 2-4 weeks"

## Annotations for stinger-forge
- Complements the AS Banka source for `guides/07-data-room-checklist.md`. The file naming convention guidance (date + version) is a concrete operational tip for the guide.
- The "cap table first" investor review priority aligns with the Command Brief's focus on cap table management as the foundational equity document.
- The 60-document number gives the stinger a specific benchmark to cite, more precise than the "50-70" range from the AS Banka source.
- Note: Peony.ink is a fundraising tool, so this content has a commercial angle. Cross-reference the document count and category structure with the AS Banka practitioner source.
